reviewer.name: Codex Reference Reviewer (Final Genesis)
reviewer.independenceStatement: I did not implement the correction under review and evaluated this stack against the pinned requirements and the architecture status registry rather than accepting implementation claims.

currentArchitectureReview.architectureStatus: Complete Draft (document edit complete; implementation evidence pending; not ratified)
currentArchitectureReview.rationale: Architecture 0.8 is a Complete Draft that is explicitly not ratified, while the Reference stack states Architecture 0.7 as its current delivery target and retains Architecture 0.5 as the implementation baseline. The plan and delivery ledger accurately label 0.7 work as partial and record the later 0.8 C1-C14 work as a future handoff, including the constraint-poisoning semantic conflict, so this assessment does not claim current-architecture conformance.

gate.notes: The exact full gate passed at the pinned commit. An initial sandboxed invocation was unable to create ordinary build obj files under the detached worktree; the identical command was rerun with workspace write access and completed successfully, including all default and cross-process test suites, builds, benchmarks, dependency checks, and assembly-boundary checks.

requirements.summary: Canonical names parse ConceptPath and AuthorityPath:ConceptPath without relative or ambiguous notation.
requirements.matrixRationale: The parser implements both canonical forms; a stable negative conformance test is not yet assigned.

review.rationale: CanonicalName parsing accepts the two architecture forms, permits only a single optional authority qualifier, validates every dotted path segment, rejects empty, relative, and ambiguous notation, and emits stable canonical text. The full gate passed; the matrix has no assigned negative snapshot, but direct source review supports the claimed parser semantics.

requirements.summary: Vocabulary versions are claims rather than semantic identity; only Shapes and Declared Fragments carry structural versions.
requirements.matrixRationale: Operation and Event identities are name-only while Shape and Fragment references carry structural versions.

positiveEvidence.anchor: public readonly record struct OperationReference(CanonicalName Name)

review.rationale: OperationReference and EventReference identities contain only CanonicalName, while ShapeReference and FragmentReference alone carry structural versions. The conformance test confirms additive same-name Shape versions and rejects incompatible redefinition, matching the architecture's identity/version boundary.

requirements.summary: Actor and authority references are issuer-controlled, unambiguous, stable, and comparable within an authority domain.
requirements.matrixRationale: Actor references are domain-issued objects with no public constructor and object-reference comparison.

review.rationale: ActorReference has no public constructor, records its issuing AuthorityDomain identifier internally, and is accepted only through issuer registry membership and object-reference identity. The positive and reflective negative tests exercise opaque issuance, stability, and comparison without caller-forgeable identity.

requirements.summary: An Execution explicitly presents a Capability whose holder, target, and Operation scope match the request at the target boundary.
requirements.matrixRationale: AuthorityDomain validates initiator, target, presented Capability, and Operation before dispatch.

review.rationale: Every public execution overload converges on target-side validation that confirms the presented Capability is registered, held by the initiator, targets the registered Operation owner, and includes the requested Operation before dispatch. The worked attack evidence verifies a mismatched production approval is rejected at that boundary.

requirements.summary: Every effective Capability Constraint is evaluated conjunctively and unknown or failed Constraints deny before effects.
requirements.matrixRationale: Effective constraints are evaluated across the derivation chain and unknown types deny.

review.rationale: EffectiveConstraints traverses the complete derivation chain and evaluates every inherited and added Constraint conjunctively before effects. Registered evaluators must validate typed values, unknown Constraint types fail closed, and the reviewed negative evidence verifies denial and provenance recording with no effect.

requirements.summary: Delegation changes the holder, preserves inherited target and Operation scope, appends Constraints, and records its parent and issuer.
requirements.matrixRationale: The derived constructor inherits target and root Operations and stores its parent.

negativeEvidence.anchor: Derivation can only change the holder and append Constraints

review.rationale: Capability derivation uses the parent grant internally, changes only the holder, inherits the original target and root Operation set, stores both parent and issuer, and appends local Constraints. Registry checks reject escaped parents, and the reviewed conformance evidence demonstrates narrowing without amplification.

requirements.summary: Primordial authority and later Genesis occurrences are bounded, enumerable, and attributable to domain policy.
requirements.matrixRationale: Genesis is callback-scoped and recorded; a failed occurrence atomically restores actors, capabilities, liveness leases, declarations, and Shape registrations. Registry membership rejects escaped references, while runtime effects and nested Genesis are blocked during the transaction.

testsRun: dotnet test .\Reference\tests\Brontide.Reference.Core.Tests\Brontide.Reference.Core.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter FullyQualifiedName~Failed_genesis_occurrence_rolls_back_renewal_of_an_existing_lease --logger "console;verbosity=minimal" (failed as expected: 1 failed; expiry 00:00:09 versus 00:00:05, outcome Succeeded, effects 1)
review.rationale: Does not conform. OccurGenesis snapshots only list counts and declaration keys; it does not snapshot mutable state inside pre-existing LivenessLease objects. Because LivenessLease.Renew is public and lacks the active-Genesis runtime guard, a callback can renew an existing lease and then throw, leaving the failed occurrence unrecorded while extending live authority. A disposable focused test reproduced expiry changing from 2030-01-01T00:00:05Z to 00:00:09Z; two seconds after the failed callback, execution was Succeeded and one effect ran instead of being Rejected with zero effects. This contradicts the matrix claim that failed occurrences atomically restore liveness leases and the pinned Finding F correction requirement.

requirements.summary: The requested effect begins only after explicit target-side authority, Shape, and Constraint validation.
requirements.matrixRationale: Handlers run after authority, Shape, and Constraint decisions.

review.rationale: AuthorityDomain performs actor, operation, capability, target, input Shape, and all Constraint decisions before invoking the registered handler. The reviewed positive flow succeeds only through checked execution, while the negative assertions retain an effects count of zero for denied requests.

requirements.summary: Rejected attempts remain observable Executions while protected command payloads are excluded from denial audit records.
requirements.matrixRationale: Rejected executions retain execution metadata and decisions in provenance, but the audit copy does not retain the protected input; the direct caller's ExecutionResult remains complete.

review.rationale: Rejected requests still produce an ExecutionResult and provenance entry containing target, operation, outcome, and Constraint decisions, but ProvenanceEntry.ForExecution stores execution.WithoutInput when authorization is false. The focused negative evidence confirms the protected input is absent from audit while the direct caller result remains complete.

requirements.summary: Trusted target time is explicit, scoped to a time domain, monotonic where claimed, and distinct from attributable sender marks.
requirements.matrixRationale: TimeProvider is explicit, sender Temporal Marks never supply trusted constraint time, and an observed-expired liveness lease remains terminally dead across clock rollback.

review.rationale: Trusted target time comes only from the AuthorityDomain TimeProvider, is labeled with the domain's clock, and sender TemporalMark data is not accepted as constraint time. Missing trusted time fails closed; LivenessLease tracks the latest observed instant and terminal death, and the tests cover expiry, non-resurrection after clock rollback, and no-clock denial.

requirements.summary: Events are immutable assertions that preserve emitter attribution, causation, declared Shape, and original temporal claims.
requirements.matrixRationale: Emitter, causation, origin, and temporal attribution are retained.

review.rationale: DomainEvent is immutable and carries an InteractionContext with emitter, occurrence, causation, origin, and temporal attribution plus a declared Shape and projected payload. The mediator replay test preserves the original emitter, and the origin negative test prevents direct origin assertion from bypassing Event semantics.

requirements.summary: An Outcome is a terminal Event for one identifiable Execution or activity, with distinct success result and failure detail Shapes.
requirements.matrixRationale: Outcomes are terminal Events and validate success and failure values under separate contracts.

review.rationale: Outcome is emitted as a terminal event for an Execution or registered activity, with duplicate terminal completion rejected. Result and failure-details contracts are validated independently, and the evidence confirms failed outcomes have no success Result while details use their distinct Shape.

requirements.summary: Shape identity is semantic and supports unit, scalar, record, sequence, choice, and opaque contracts without CLR assignability.
requirements.matrixRationale: All Base Shape kinds are represented and semantic identity does not use CLR assignability.

review.rationale: ShapeRegistry represents unit, scalar, record, sequence, choice, and opaque definitions and validates immutable ShapeValue structures by semantic ShapeReference rather than CLR assignability. The reviewed tests cover all base kinds and reject authority-bearing or incorrectly typed scalar carriers.

requirements.summary: Same-name Shape versions are monotonic and additive; breaking structure or meaning requires a new canonical name.
requirements.matrixRationale: Later same-name Shape definitions are checked for additive compatibility.

review.rationale: ShapeRegistry compares same-name versions against prior definitions and permits only monotonic additive evolution with compatible meaning and structure. The positive and negative evidence exercises valid addition and throws ShapeRegistrationException for incompatible same-name redefinition.

requirements.summary: Open Shapes accept valid authored Fragments through canonical projection, required Fragments fail closed, and closed Shapes reject attachment.
requirements.matrixRationale: Open, required, projected, and closed Fragment behavior has executable evidence.

review.rationale: Projection implements open, required, optional, transparent-forwarding, and closed Fragment behavior: authored Fragments are validated against registered contracts, required Fragments fail closed, canonical ordering is applied, and closed Shapes reject attachments. The reviewed evidence covers optional open projection and closed-shape rejection.

requirements.summary: Cross-stack component evidence crosses an external boundary without sharing implementation-private runtime types or authority objects.
requirements.matrixRationale: The retained full gate launches the independently built Minimal provider process for Cooling and Catalog/resource proofs. This is experimental interchange evidence, not protocol ratification.

review.rationale: The Reference host exchanges portable JSON envelopes and manifests with an independently built Minimal provider executable for Cooling and Catalog scenarios. Project, dependency, and assembly checks prohibit private Minimal runtime references, and the cross-process tests cover success, forwarding, provenance, nested batches, two operations, and explicit failures without transporting authority objects.

attestation.statement: I attest that this record accurately reports my independent review of the pinned commit and the architecture selected by the status registry.
